theory.intro: When angles sit side by side along a straight line, they fill a half turn, so together they add to $180^{\circ}$. These are called **supplementary** angles.
facts: Adjacent angles on a **straight line** add to $180^{\circ}$.
A straight line is a half turn, which measures $180^{\circ}$.
If one angle is known, subtract it from $180^{\circ}$ to find the other.
steps: Check the angles sit along a straight line (a half turn).
